trivia_bullets.text: Modern Horizons 3 (MH3, released 2024-06-14) revives Replicate — originally an Izzet keyword from Guildpact (2006, Ravnica block) — now rewired to spend Energy counters instead of mana. Replicate in Guildpact appeared on 10 cards, all in Izzet colors; MH3 re-contextualizes it in an Energy shell for the blue-red archetype. [Scryfall; Draftsim.com: MTG Replicate mechanic history via WebSearch]
sources: Draftsim.com: MTG Replicate mechanic history (WebSearch snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "Eureka! Now to show those fools at the Academy how easily I can replicate *these* results!" [Scryfall] The unnamed speaker's academy-rivalry rhetoric is consistent with the Izzet guild's institutional culture on Ravnica — the League competes directly with the Azorius Senate and Simic Combine for intellectual prestige. The card is tagged in the corpus with characters: ["izzet-league"].

Reiterating Bolt deals 3 damage to a creature or planeswalker for {1}{R}, then can be copied for each additional {E}{E}{E} spent (Energy Replicate). In MH3 Limited, the blue-red Energy archetype generates Energy via cantrips and generators; copying this bolt 2-3 times in one turn becomes a plausible board-clear sequence. EDHREC rank 17,009 reflects niche Commander interest. [Scryfall; dotesports.com: Best MH3 draft removal via WebSearch]

Illustrated by G-host Lee, whose work appears across multiple recent Magic sets. The original Replicate keyword was designed with modularity in mind — the design team intentionally separated the replicate cost from the casting cost so it could return with different cost parameters in future sets; MH3's energy-cost version is the realization of that flexibility. [Draftsim.com: Replicate history via WebSearch snippet]
